interpretation: The island observations belong to var. platyphyllum, a botanical variety distinct from the mainland comparator.
boundary: Do not treat this as a within-variety island effect or an independent Bombus-loss replicate.

pollinator_context: diurnal swallowtails and nocturnal hawkmoths emphasized
interpretation: The mainland observations belong to var. auratum.
boundary: Variety identity and geography are inseparable in this design.

interpretation: The source describes the island variety as having 25-30 cm flowers.
boundary: This is a taxonomic/natural-history description, not a standardized population mean or effect size.

interpretation: The source describes the mainland variety as having 8-20 cm flowers.
boundary: Do not score this description as a quantitative island effect.

reported_test: fruit and seed set from selfing lower than outcrossing; p<0.05 or p<0.01 as marked
interpretation: Both varieties were interpreted as self-incompatible and therefore pollinator-dependent.
boundary: This does not measure realized outcrossing rate or a Bombus-specific breeding-system transition.

n: site-year sample sizes shown in Figure 5
reported_test: open seed set lower than hand outcrossing in several site-years; fruit set not significantly different
interpretation: The authors interpret lower open-pollinated seed set as pollen limitation.
boundary: Site-year significance is heterogeneous; do not collapse to a single island effect.

site_or_group: Izu-Oshima spotted n=5; Izu-Oshima unspotted n=3; Aogashima unspotted n=5
interpretation: Island-variety scent intensity rose in the evening and at night, often with two daily peaks.
boundary: Odor-sensor units are relative; this is a timing pattern, not an absolute cross-study scent effect size.

interpretation: Mainland-variety scent was low during the day and highest at night during the first two days.
boundary: The study did not randomize variety independently of geography.

reported_test: descriptive time series; no variety-level inferential test
interpretation: Nectar volume increased during the night; maximum 85.38 uL at 23:00 on day 1.
boundary: Small n and unreplicated site-by-variety comparison; use as source-native mechanism context only.

reported_test: descriptive time series; no variety-level inferential test
interpretation: Nectar volume increased only slightly at night; maximum 44.69 uL at 23:00 on day 2.
boundary: Do not treat the maximum difference as an effect size.

interpretation: All 62 Lepidoptera visits to spotted Oshima flowers occurred at night.
boundary: Raw events are not independent evolutionary replicates and camera exposure differs among groups.

interpretation: Eighty of 82 Lepidoptera visits to unspotted Oshima flowers occurred at night.
boundary: The authors report no difference in visitors or visiting frequency between spotted and unspotted flowers.

pollinator_context: daytime small hawkmoths/butterflies plus nocturnal large hawkmoths
interpretation: Sixty-six of 151 Lepidoptera visits occurred at night and 85 during the day.
boundary: Aogashima differs from Oshima within the island variety; this is not a simple island-wide state.

pollinator_context: diurnal swallowtails and nocturnal large hawkmoths
interpretation: The authors identify both diurnal swallowtails and nocturnal large hawkmoths as important mainland pollinators.
boundary: This is a Lepidoptera-guild comparison, not a Bombus-regime test.

n: day seed-set n=4; night n=9; open n=6
interpretation: Seed set from nocturnal pollination was significantly higher than from diurnal pollination.
boundary: This supports nocturnal visitor effectiveness on Oshima, not staged Bombus loss.

reported_test: variety identity confounded with geography; focal guild is Lepidoptera
interpretation: Retain as a source-native example of an alternative island pollination mechanism; exclude from the primary Bombus two-threshold score.
boundary: It may inform an alternative-guild discussion or future stratified synthesis, but cannot validate the specialist Bombus holdout.
